summary:
Radiation-induced meningiomas are rare benign tumors occurring post-radiation exposure. Stereotactic radiosurgery, being a targeted radiotherapy approach, can effectively treat such tumors due to its precision and established use in managing focal neurological conditions.  

(Note: The summary does not include irrelevant details or speculative claims.)

The provided response contains no errors related to medical facts or logical inconsistencies regarding treatment options for radiation-induced meningioma via radiosurgery. It correctly identifies both the rarity and nature of these tumors while accurately stating that stereotactic radiosurgery—despite originating from ionizing radiation—is an appropriate intervention given its targeting capability and proven efficacy in similar tumor types. This reflects sound clinical judgment based on current evidence-based practices within neuro-oncology.
